creator: accounts@wireme.de
text:
I can confirm that problem using rocksdb.

I think that the update introduced the following changes, which can require manual intervention:

1. the database path name seems to have changed from `/var/db/conduit` to `/var/lib/matrix-conduit`

2. There is now a conduit user account and group and I believe that makes fixing permissions with `chown [-R] conduit:conduit [paths]` for the database path and the config.

3. To avoid running conduit as root (which could lead in files to be created with wrong permissions again, I suggest to use the following command for further testing: `su -m conduit -c 'env RUST_BACKTRACE=full CONDUIT_CONFIG=/usr/local/etc/conduit.toml /usr/local/bin/conduit'`

My observations so far:

1. setting `log = "debug"` in `/usr/local/etc/conduit.toml` shows me the error message from [here](https://gitlab.com/famedly/conduit/-/blob/next/src/main.rs?ref_type=heads#L148) on the console:

```
2024-10-12T09:46:38.001130Z DEBUG maximize_fd_limit: conduit: Current nofile soft limit: 234702
2024-10-12T09:46:38.001175Z DEBUG maximize_fd_limit: conduit: Increased nofile soft limit to 234702
2024-10-12T09:46:38.001184Z  INFO conduit: Loading database
Segmentation fault
```

2. clearing the database folder creates a new database and the crash is gone (so are your data)

3. An attempt to repair the rocksdb database with `ldb repair --db=/var/lib/matrix-conduit/` returns `Some data may have been lost.`:

```
2024/10/12-11:55:02.268835 275c77e12000 [WARN] [/version_set.cc:4667] [roomuserid_invitecount]CF's epoch numbers are inferred based on seqno
2024/10/12-11:55:02.270964 275c77e12000 [WARN] [/version_set.cc:4667] [shortstatehash_statediff]CF's epoch numbers are inferred based on seqno
2024/10/12-11:55:02.284139 275c77e12000 [WARN] [/repair.cc:243] **** Repaired rocksdb /var/lib/matrix-conduit/; recovered 1227 files; 1372312906 bytes. Some data may have been lost. ****
OK
```
